verdictDefinitions.accept: claim correctly extracted and all agent labels correct
verdictDefinitions.relabel: claim correctly extracted but one or more labels corrected (proof route, audience, epic, etc.; correction in note)
verdictDefinitions.not-a-claim: text should not have been extracted as a behavior claim
verdictDefinitions.badly-bounded: real claim but wrong excerpt boundary, wrong merge, or duplicate (detail in note)
verdictDefinitions.retire-source: extraction correct, but the maintainer removes the source sentence (claim leaves the gold set; source-edit recorded for a later doc pass + re-anchor)
verdictDefinitions.rewrite-source: extraction correct, but the maintainer simplifies/rewrites the source sentence (claim essence kept but trimmed; source-edit recorded)

entries.summary: Document HITL review reads the rendered preview, not just raw markdown: when chunk-reviewing public docs or spec prose, `npm run docs:preview:changed` is run and the width snapshot under `.artifacts/markdown-preview/` is read; an empty snapshot is treated as a renderer seam bug rather than accepting exit-0 as sufficient.

entries.summary: Live app discovery/invocation is proven first in JS and graduated to Go once the operator-facing entrypoint is agreed; after graduation the consumer-owned seams remain (kind: command discovery still has the consumer probe emit the catalog, and live invocation still uses the adapter's consumer_command_template for actual launch/runtime wiring) while the Go command fixes the product-owned packet contract, validation, and dispatch rules as the official surface.

agentLabels.edgeRationale: E1-evaluate is supported because live consumer-target discovery/invocation feeds the evaluate execution surface; S1-install is supported because graduating the entrypoint to the Go binary fixes the installed operator-facing CLI surface.
